title: Auth — JWT Refresh Token Rotation
description: Implementation plan for adding refresh token rotation to the auth module. Green nodes are new; amber nodes are modified. Click any node to see its signature and code.

nodes.label: API Client
nodes.description: Caller of the auth endpoints — a web or mobile client.

nodes.label: auth_router.py
nodes.description: FastAPI router for /auth/* endpoints. Registers login and the new token refresh route.

nodes.label: issue_token_pair()
nodes.description: Creates both an access token and a refresh token, persists the refresh token to the DB, and returns both to the caller.

nodes.label: rotate_refresh_token()
nodes.description: Validates the incoming refresh token, deletes it (single-use), issues a new token pair. Raises 401 if the token is unknown or expired.
nodes.signature: def rotate_refresh_token(db: Session, raw_token: str) -> TokenPair

nodes.label: create_access_token()
nodes.description: Encodes a short-lived JWT. Unchanged — called by the new issue_token_pair helper.

nodes.label: hash_token()
nodes.description: SHA-256 hashes a raw token string before DB storage so plaintext tokens are never persisted.
nodes.code_snippet:
def hash_token(raw: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(raw.encode()).hexdigest()

nodes.label: RefreshToken
nodes.description: SQLAlchemy model for the refresh_tokens table. Each row is a single-use token tied to a user.
nodes.code_snippet:
class RefreshToken(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'refresh_tokens'
    id         = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id    = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    token      = Column(String(64), unique=True, nullable=False)  # SHA-256 hex
    expires_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=utcnow)

nodes.label: refresh_tokens table
nodes.description: Stores hashed refresh tokens. Old tokens are deleted on use (rotation) or by a nightly cleanup job.

sequences.label: Before — login returns access token only
